Title: Chemical inhibition of INO1 reduces phytic acid in rice and wheat grains for enhanced micronutrient bioavailability

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during seed development, especially embryo; peaks around 6–10 days after flowering/pollination in rice seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rice seeds/panicles at developing grain stage (DAF 6–12 treatment window); wheat ortholog peak expression during seed development around DAF 14
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Myo-inositol-3-phosphate synthase (MIPS) family; NAD-dependent enzyme; tetrameric
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core committed entry step of phytic acid biosynthesis via myo-inositol phosphate formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glucose 6-phosphate into myo-inositol 3-phosphate in an NAD-dependent cyclization/isomerization reaction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Mineral nutrient antinutrient / phosphorus storage metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phosphorus storage compound in cereal grains; chelates iron and zinc and lowers intestinal mineral absorption; also supports seed nutrient supply during germination and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; 19F-NMR fragment screening; Lineweaver-Burk inhibition analysis; isothermal titration calorimetry; alanine-substitution mutagenesis; docking simulation; in planta fragment spraying; metabolite and mineral profiling; comparison with Tos17 insertion mutant
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Spray treatment of hit fragments to developing panicles/spikes during seed development (rice DAF 6, 8, 10, 12); greenhouse conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Chemical inhibition of INO1 reduced phytic acid content in rice and wheat grains without reducing Fe or Zn content, thereby increasing mineral-to-PA ratio and predicted micronutrient bioavailability
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key control point and first step determining PA content in cereal grains
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Genetic disruption of INO1 severely impaired germination, seedling growth, embryo development and yield-related traits; chemical inhibition avoided obvious penalties under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for low-phytic-acid crop development; chemical antagonists such as PK0-28011 provide a non-genetic route applicable across cereals and may be optimized for field use
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression information referenced from RiceXpro database; protein structure deposited as PDB 9M53
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rice INO1 shares >95% amino-acid similarity with wheat INO1 and >85% identity with several major crops; active-site residue K468 appears important for PK0-28011 interaction; Tos17 insertion mutant caused ~38% PA decrease but strong yield penalties; chemical treatment achieved up to 31% reduction in rice and up to 47% in wheat seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. INO1 encodes the first committed enzyme in phytic acid biosynthesis and strongly determines seed PA content. 2. Eight fragment hits inhibited recombinant rice INO1 by mixed inhibition, with PK0-28011 showing evidence of active-site interaction involving K468. 3. Spraying hit fragments onto developing rice and wheat reproductive organs significantly reduced grain PA without lowering Fe or Zn contents. 4. Compared with genetic disruption, transient chemical inhibition of INO1 avoided obvious defects in yield, seed viability, and grain quality.
